extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won after JUMBO-VISMA occupied all three front positions entering the Paterberg at 36km to go, splitting the race decisively with only LAPORTE able to follow.
tactical_patterns: JUMBO-VISMA's triple front occupation entering Paterberg (36km out) created race-winning split despite 20-rider lead group existing moments before.
LAPORTE sacrificing individual result by bridging to VAN AERT rather than contesting third enabled perfect team 1-2 execution.
INEOS bringing gap down specifically to enable LEVIOUS bridge attempt shows desperation tactics when lacking pure climbers for Berg selections.
Chase group organizing rotations over cobbled climbs proves futile when deficit reaches 20 seconds post-Paterberg, suggesting critical threshold for irreversible splits.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Second time this season executing 2-rider breakaway with teammate from world-class field reveals unprecedented dominance pattern.
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: Fifth place at age 21 in debut cobbled classic marks him as legitimate threat for future monument contention.
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: negative: Defending champion unable to follow Paterberg acceleration and relegated to chase group suggests form decline.
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: positive: Solo attack from chase group for third shows others lacked sprint confidence, tactical opportunity identification.
rider_signals.LEVIOUS Jonathan: neutral: Successfully bridged 80km effort but dropped on Paterberg reveals excellent diesel engine but insufficient explosive power for decisive climbs.
